scope: Scope: Customer onboarding (plan selection, identity verification, payment, order creation) and Customer self-care (usage, billing, plan change, profile, portability). Excludes support tickets, static

modules.name: pml-my

key_abstractions.name: Customer (Onboarding)

properties.name: metadata
invariants: metadata.verified = false — no Order has completed

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: MSISDN must be reserved from inventory before Cart advances

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: only Plans with price > 0 and isSellable = true are selectable

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: Persona KYC inquiry must complete successfully before profile submission

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: card data never passes through the application

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: requires a completed Transaction and an existing Billing account before activation
invariants: POST /billing creates billing.id; POST /order sets metadata.verified = true

operations.name: selectSim
invariants: pSIM requires a valid ICCID; eSIM does not

operations.name: portNumber
invariants: portability.verified = true required before Cart can advance

operations.name: update
invariants: when account type is Enterprise, identity intersects with EnterpriseIdentity

properties.name: verified
invariants: verified = false means no Order has completed; route gate checks billing.id

references.title: useFormUserData
references.locator: profile submission with Persona data merge

decisions: Two Customer aggregates are modeled as distinct domain objects — the lifecycle transition is a meaningful business event (Order creation); the code has two separate type files with no inheritance (DDR-001).
Identity is a concept, not a property — it has its own update path (`patchCustomer({ identity })`), its own KYC boundary interaction, and an intersection subtype (EnterpriseIdentity).
Address is a concept — own update path, own Google Maps boundary interaction, own field structure.
Metadata is a concept — `verified` is the cross-cutting lifecycle invariant; `type` disambiguates consumer vs enterprise rendering.
OnboardingCustomer operations map 1:1 to onboarding steps: selectNumber → selectPlan → selectSim → submitProfile → submitPayment → activate.

key_abstractions.name: Customer (Self-Care)

properties.name: billing
invariants: billing.id must be present (route gate: `!!customer?.billing?.id`)

properties.name: metadata
invariants: metadata.verified = true — Order has completed

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: uses same FAC iframe mechanism as onboarding; afterPaymentGoToAtom routes back

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: settles current billing.balance via card charge

operations.name: selectSubscription
invariants: switches active line in multi-subscription accounts

operations.name: updateProfile
invariants: patchCustomer PATCH merges payload optimistically into customerAtom

operations.name: submitPortability
invariants: porting an additional number onto the account

references.title: subscriptionSelector
references.locator: `subscriptionSelector` — selects first subscription or by stored ID

references.title: useChangePlan
references.locator: plan change flow with roaming ticket gate

decisions: SelfCareCustomer has `billing` as required (rich shape with invoices, balance, transactions); OnboardingCustomer has `billing` as optional (thin shape with id + defaultPayment only).
`selectSubscription` is a Self-Care action — the UI allows switching the active line when a Customer holds multiple Subscriptions.
`changePlan` produces an Order — it is the same business event as onboarding activation but for an existing customer changing their enrolled Bundle.
`updatePaymentMethod` and `payOutstandingBalance` both use Payment but serve different purposes — one updates the stored card, the other settles a balance.

key_abstractions.name: Subscription

properties.name: status
invariants: status ≠ active means Usage is inaccessible

decisions: Bundle is subordinate to Subscription — it is the snapshot of Plan terms at order time; it does not update when the Catalog changes.
Bundle is distinct from Plan — Plan is a catalog offering; Bundle is what is enrolled on a Subscription.
Usage is a concept — carries its own null-quota invariant and two named sub-dimensions.
SIMType is a value object (constrained enum) — associated with both Cart and Subscription.

key_abstractions.name: Catalog

operations.name: fetch
invariants: fetched once per session (staleTime: Infinity); unauthenticated; cached in TanStack Query

operations.name: filter
invariants: only Plans with price > 0 and name not containing ' PROMO' suffix are presented

properties.name: isSellable
invariants: price > 0 and isSellable = true to appear in the Catalog

operations.name: reserve
invariants: POST to inventory/msisdn reserves the number; writes msisdn to Cart

operations.name: search
invariants: Keyword is up to 5 characters; searches available MSISDN inventory

references.title: useCatalog
references.locator: `staleTime: Infinity`, fetch once per session, filter price > 0

decisions: Catalog is the collection KA; Plan is the primary class within it — aligns with code and TM Forum standard.
Feature is a property of Plan — no independent behavior or invariants.
NumberOption belongs under Catalog KA — it is an inventory item surfaced alongside Plan selection.
NumberOption has `reserve` and `search` operations — these are domain actions that interact with Cart.

key_abstractions.name: Cart

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: after Order creation, Cart is stale — selections superseded by Subscription

operations.name: patchNumber
invariants: writes msisdn from reserved NumberOption or verified Portability

operations.name: patchPlan
invariants: extracts bundleId from Plan; merges with existing cart state

operations.name: patchSim
invariants: pSIM requires valid iccid; eSIM ignores iccid

properties.name: verified
invariants: verified = true required before Cart can advance past LineNumber step

operations.name: verify
invariants: triggered only when PORTING_2FA flag is enabled; when disabled, Portability proceeds without SMS

operations.name: resend
invariants: re-sends verification code to the port number

operations.name: apply
invariants: applies discount to Cart total at checkout; reverts on invalid

decisions: Cart is owned by OnboardingCustomer — it is the working memory of onboarding, has its own PATCH endpoint, and has a distinct stale-after-order lifecycle.
Portability is subordinate to Cart — own fields, own SMS 2FA sub-flow, own verified-gate invariant.
PortabilityVerification is subordinate to Portability — own precondition (flag), own verify/resend actions.
Voucher is subordinate to Cart — validated against midtier before applied; modifies Cart total.
Cart operations are granular per step: patchNumber, patchPlan, patchSim — each corresponds to an onboarding step.

key_abstractions.name: Billing

properties.name: transactions
invariants: a Billing account exists if and only if at least one Order has been successfully created

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: POST /billing with transactionId; creates billing.id — the route gate for Self-Care access

properties.name: status
invariants: must be present before recurring charges can be collected

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: uses same iframe mechanism as onboarding Payment; afterPaymentGoToAtom routes back

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: requires a completed Transaction and existing Billing account
invariants: POST /order sets metadata.verified = true — transitions OnboardingCustomer to SelfCareCustomer
billing.id is the route gate (Protected.tsx checks `!!customer?.billing?.id`)

decisions: Billing is a separate KA from Payment — Billing owns the financial account and history; Payment owns card capture mechanics.
Order is placed under Billing KA — it creates the Billing account and activates the Subscription; its outcome is a financial and lifecycle event.
PaymentMethod is a concept under Billing — has own display representation, independently updatable via FAC iframe.
Billing.create is a distinct operation from Order.create — billing account is created first (POST /billing), then order is placed (POST /order).

key_abstractions.name: Payment

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: sequential: POST /billing → POST /order; both must succeed for activation

operations.name: onIframeReply
invariants: on 'success' reply, triggers Transaction status poll; on 'error', increments attempt count

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: GET /payment/status/:transactionId; completed is precondition for Order creation

properties.name: maxAttempts
invariants: maxAttempts controlled by GrowthBook PAYMENT_ATTEMPTS flag; default 3

properties.name: Invariant
properties.return_type: POST /payment/failed; navigates to Done with failure state; no further retries permitted

operations.name: retry
invariants: reloads FAC iframe for another attempt

references.title: usePayment (My)
references.locator: payment method update variant (same iframe mechanism, no order creation)

decisions: Payment is a separate KA from Billing — Payment owns card capture mechanics and retry logic; Billing owns the financial account record.
Transaction is a first-class concept — has a gateway-assigned ID, a three-state status lifecycle, and a polled query path.
PaymentAttempt is a concept — has its own counter, configurable maximum (GrowthBook flag), terminal failure path, and session invariant.
Payment.createOrder orchestrates the full activation sequence — this is the domain event that transitions OnboardingCustomer to SelfCareCustomer.

operations.name: getAuthorizationHeader
invariants: force-refreshes session; returns idtoken + Bearer authorization

operations.name: onComplete
invariants: inquiry must complete successfully before profile submission

operations.name: fetchDocument
invariants: retrieves ID document data for expiry date mapping

operations.name: load
invariants: GET /payment/auth returns HTML rendered as iframe srcdoc

operations.name: submitForm
invariants: parent sends postMessage('submitForm', '*'); no origin restriction

operations.name: onError
invariants: card data never leaves the FAC iframe; application never handles raw card numbers

operations.name: createCustomer
invariants: POST /customer; called from ensureCustomerExists when Cognito lacks customerId

decisions: All boundary placements: external regulated services, authentication, feature flags, payment card capture, address autocomplete — none are core domain responsibilities.
Midtier API is one boundary representing the Paradise Mobile backend; it is the integration point for all Customer, Cart, Billing, Order, Payment, and Catalog operations.
Cognito.getAuthorizationHeader is a boundary operation — every authenticated midtier call must manually invoke it (no interceptors).
Midtier API.createCustomer is a provisioning operation — called only once at sign-up when Cognito lacks the customerId attribute.

modules.intro:
Scope: Customer onboarding (plan selection, identity verification, payment, order creation) and Customer self-care (usage, billing, plan change, profile, portability). Excludes support tickets, static marketing pages.

**Core terms**:
- Onboarding Customer, Self-Care Customer, Identity, EnterpriseIdentity, Address, Metadata
- Subscription, Bundle, SIMType, Usage
- Catalog, Plan, NumberOption
- Cart, Portability, PortabilityVerification, Voucher
- Billing, Invoice, PaymentMethod, Order
- Payment, Transaction, PaymentAttempt

**Key Abstractions (term grouping)**:
- **Onboarding Customer**: the pre-activation aggregate — owns Cart, Identity, Address, Metadata (`verified = false`). Source: `Onboarding/types/Customer.ts`. See DDR-001.
- **Self-Care Customer**: the post-activation aggregate — owns Subscriptions, full Billing, enriched Identity, Metadata (`verified = true`). Source: `My/types/Customer.ts`. See DDR-001.
- **Subscription**: an active mobile service line with Bundle, SIMType, and Usage
- **Catalog**: the set of sellable Plans; includes NumberOption inventory
- **Cart**: the onboarding workspace accumulating an Onboarding Customer's selections before Order creation
- **Billing**: the Self-Care Customer's financial account — invoices, balance, payment method, Order history
- **Order**: the business event that activates a customer (flips `metadata.verified` to `true`, transitions Onboarding Customer → Self-Care Customer)
- **Payment**: the card-capture act delegated to the FAC iframe boundary

> **Domain decision DDR-001**: The production code has two structurally divergent `Customer` type files (`Onboarding/types/Customer.ts` and `My/types/Customer.ts`). These are two separate domain aggregates with no shared base type — they are structurally independent definitions that both write to the same `customerAtom`. See `docs/domain/decisions/DDR-001-disconnected-customer-aggregates-lack-shared-inheritance.md`.
